Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tripwire mode for automatic stuck recovery

--tripwire flag monitors Claude and auto-restarts when stuck:
- Checks health score every N seconds (default: 30)
- After 2 consecutive scores >= 50, kills and relaunches Claude
- 120s cooldown after restart to let new session stabilize
- Max 5 restarts per session as safety cap
- All restarts logged to health-log.txt with TRIPWIRE/RESTART tags

Usage: claude-health.exe --tripwire 30
       claude-health.exe --tripwire 30 --strikes 3 --threshold 40

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve infinite re-render loop in CompanyDetailModal

Zustand selector was calling .filter() which returns a new array
reference on every snapshot call. React 19's useSyncExternalStore
detects this as an unstable snapshot and triggers infinite re-renders.

Fix: select full scheduledActions array from store (stable reference),
filter by companyId in component body instead of inside the selector.

commit.message:
fix: Selective backup restore data loss + Bluetooth wizard tab navigation

- Fix backup restore deleting data from tables NOT in the backup archive.
  Root cause: when all group checkboxes were selected, frontend sent
  groups=null causing backend to DELETE ALL tables, but only INSERT
  tables present in the archive. Now frontend always sends selected
  groups, and backend intersects allowed tables with actual archive
  contents (double safety layer).
- Enable FK cascade prevention (session_replication_role=replica) for
  any partial restore, not just explicitly selected groups.
- Bluetooth wizard: all step tabs clickable after BLE connection,
  enabling direct access to factory reset/reboot without going through
  WiFi/protocol steps. Before connection, only reached steps clickable.
- Add Arabic (ar) backup.json translation.
- Fix duplicate NotificationTriggers method, add tenant backup routes.

Fix elite accumulation, canJoinPopulation timing, mutation base, and add difficulty weighting

- markElite: clear all project elites before marking new one to prevent unbounded elite accumulation
- addPrompt: add isActive param; evolve inserts variants as inactive, activates only after qualifying
- evolve: use annotated targetPromptId as mutation base instead of always population[0]
- evaluateBatch: add runs param to average scores across N evaluations (uses evaluationRuns config)
- computeWeightedAvgScore: weight test case scores by difficulty (easy=0.5, medium=1.0, hard=1.5)
- getTestCases: filter by isActive=true and include difficulty field in both server and cli

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Privacy enhancement: Aggregate map by ZIP code instead of exact locations

Changed map view to show reports grouped by ZIP code for better privacy:

Features:
- Reports aggregated by ZIP code (not exact addresses)
- Circular markers sized by number of reports in each ZIP
- Number badge on each marker showing report count
- Click marker to see ZIP code summary with breakdown by category/brand
- List of reports in that ZIP (without exact addresses)
- Privacy notice banner explaining ZIP-level display
- Filters still work (category, brand)

New API endpoint:
- GET /api/reports/map/zipcode - Returns aggregated data by ZIP code
- Includes report counts, category breakdown, brand breakdown
- Uses first report's location as ZIP center approximation

Benefits:
- Protects user privacy (no exact addresses shown)
- Better overview of problem areas
- Easier to identify regional patterns
- Complies with privacy best practices

commit.message:
Resolve all open questions in roadmap — 10 definitive decisions

D1: Shared DB at ~/.pando/council/council.db (same-process SQLite is safe)
D2: Board tasks replace findings — title convention [SEVERITY:CATEGORY]
D3: Council projectPath = node repo root (builder sub-agents work directly)
D4: Three agents now, scale via EnginePool later
D5: No cross-node council coordination (each node independent)
D6: No authority levels (governance is the gate)
D7: Rollback via natural QA feedback loop
D8: Governance for ecosystem repos only, user projects skip
D9: Agent profiles via shared DB insert
D10: One engine per agent, scheduler addresses by engineId

commit.message:
Add PostGIS integration tests for walk-way matching logic

Tests the ST_DWithin + ST_Length(ST_Intersection(...)) > 20m filter
directly against the real DB using inline geometry (no table reads,
no test data cleanup needed):

- Perpendicular cross-street: ~16m overlap → not credited ✓
- Parallel road within 8m: ~76m overlap → credited ✓
- Road outside 8m buffer: 0m overlap → not credited ✓
- Short road < 20m alongside walk: documents known regression where
  very short ways won't be credited even if fully walked

commit.message:
Add auth PRD, PRD registry, update CLAUDE.md workflow

PRD-auth.md:
- Covers full OAuth user account system (Google + GitHub)
- D1 schema: users + sessions tables, user_id FK on cats
- Worker: /api/auth/* routes, session middleware scoping all queries
- Frontend: AuthContext, /login page, ProtectedRoute, user avatar in nav
- Data migration: claim-on-first-login prompt for existing cats
- Open questions documented for product owner review
- Status: Under Review (do not implement until Approved in REGISTRY.md)

REGISTRY.md:
- Canonical status tracker for all PRDs
- Statuses: Draft → Under Review → Approved → In Progress → Implemented / Rejected / Superseded
- Documents which items from each PRD are implemented vs pending
- Includes "Planned (no PRD yet)" section for known future work
- Replaces ad-hoc status tracking in individual PRD files

CLAUDE.md:
- Working style step 1 now reads REGISTRY.md before TODO.md
- PRD section replaced with registry-first workflow instructions
- PRD status table (quick reference without opening the registry)
- Auth "What NOT to do" updated: blocked until PRD-auth.md is Approved

commit.message:
Replace face fill with concentric ring distribution and bump MAX_SPLATS to 256

Rewrote face interior fill to use 8 concentric rings interpolated between
face center and contour, giving even coverage by construction. Removed
landmark-based fill (MediaPipe landmarks cluster on nose/eyes, leaving
forehead empty) and rim splats (caused busy eye/mouth areas). Ring points
track face movement since they derive from tracked contour positions.

commit.message:
Add reference codec CLI for validating against the JS implementation

Node.js CLI wrapper (codec/refcodec.js) loads the vendored Stage Games
JavaScript codec via vm.createContext without modifying the reference
directory. Supports decode and encode subcommands with file or stdin
input. Output uses the .decoded test file format (type line + JSON).

Documented in manual and project memory with emphasis that the
reference JS codec is the authoritative specification — our Go codec
must always match its output.
